name: Ron DeSantis
summary: Ron DeSantis has consistently demonstrated a pattern of behavior that prioritizes conservative ideologies and restricts individual rights. He has supported and implemented policies that limit access to abortion, restrict voting rights, and target marginalized communities, including LGBTQ+ individuals and immigrants. DeSantis has also shown a willingness to use his executive power to punish those who oppose him, as seen in his actions against Disney and his efforts to restrict free speech. Additionally, he has been involved in controversies surrounding racial issues, including his use of language that has been interpreted as a racist dog whistle. Overall, DeSantis's actions reveal a commitment to advancing a conservative agenda, often at the expense of vulnerable populations and democratic principles.

entries.fact: Ron DeSantis advised the commander of the Guantanamo Bay detention camp about the use of force-feeding of detainees.
DeSantis voted in favor of H.R. 45, which would have repealed the Affordable Care Act.
DeSantis signed a 'No Climate Tax Pledge' against any tax hikes to fight global warming.
DeSantis introduced a bill that would have required the Justice Department to report to Congress whenever any federal agency refrained from enforcing laws.
DeSantis co-sponsored Kate's Law, which would have increased penalties for aliens who unlawfully reenter the U.S. after being removed.
DeSantis spoke at the Hudson Institute and expressed his opposition to tax hikes to fight global warming.
DeSantis received a '0' rating from the Human Rights Campaign on LGBT-related legislation.
DeSantis introduced the Higher Education Reform and Opportunity Act, which would have allowed states to create their own accreditation systems.
DeSantis was present before the congressional baseball shooting and the perpetrator asked him whether the players were Republicans.
DeSantis proposed legislation that would have ended funding for the Mueller investigation of President Trump.
DeSantis voted for the 2017 Trump tax cuts.

DeSantis said, 'The last thing we need to do is to monkey this up by trying to embrace a socialist agenda with huge tax increases and bankrupting the state,' in a televised Fox News interview, which was interpreted by some as a racist dog whistle alluding to the Democratic gubernatorial nominee, Andrew Gillum, who is African-American.
DeSantis posthumously pardoned the Groveland Four, a group of black men falsely convicted of rape in 1949.

DeSantis officially suspended Broward County sheriff Scott Israel, ostensibly for his responses to the mass shootings at the Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School, appointing Gregory Tony to replace him.
DeSantis signed a $91.1 billion budget, the largest in state history at the time, though he cut $131 million in appropriations.

DeSantis signed a measure that would make it harder to launch successful ballot initiatives, affecting petition-gathering for ballot initiatives to legalize medical cannabis, increases to the minimum wage, and expansion of Medicaid
DeSantis signed an anti-'sanctuary city' bill into law, requiring local governments to aid federal immigration-enforcement efforts.
DeSantis ordered the Florida Department of Economic Opportunity to extend unemployment waivers until February 27, 2021.

DeSantis announced his support for eliminating ballot drop boxes and limiting voting by mail by requiring that voters re-register every year to vote by mail and that signatures on mail-in ballots 'match the most recent signature on file'
Ron DeSantis announced support for legislation to hold tech companies accountable for alleged political censorship, in response to social media networks removing Trump from their platforms.
DeSantis signed a $101.5 billion budget, using his line-item veto to veto $1.5 billion, including $1 billion in federal American Rescue Plan Act money for an emergency response fund.
DeSantis led an effort to ban the teaching of critical race theory in Florida public schools, describing it as 'teaching kids to hate their country'
Ron DeSantis announced he would transport illegal immigrants to Democratic states, a decision criticized by Charlie Crist as a human rights abuse.
DeSantis announced that he would temporarily waive Florida's gasoline tax in the next legislative session, in 2022, due to a significant increase in gasoline prices.

DeSantis announced a new bill, the Stop Wrongs to Our Kids and Employees Act, which would allow parents to sue school districts that teach critical race theory, framing it as a bill to combat 'woke indoctrination' that would 'teach our kids to hate our country or hate each other'

Ron DeSantis signed into law a bill that bans abortions after 15 weeks of pregnancy, with exceptions for physical health risks or fatal fetal abnormalities, but no exceptions for rape, human trafficking, incest, or mental health.
Federal judge Mark E. Walker blocked enforcement of the Stop WOKE Act as applied to businesses, ruling that it violated the First Amendment and was impermissibly vague
Ron DeSantis referred to Charlie Crist as 'a worn-out old donkey' during the gubernatorial debate.
Ron DeSantis won the Florida gubernatorial election with 59.4 percent of the vote, the largest margin of victory in a Florida gubernatorial election since 1982.
Ron DeSantis signed a six-week abortion ban, which requires women to provide proof of a crime before being permitted an abortion in cases of rape, incest, or human trafficking.
DeSantis signed a bill requiring government employers and contractors to use E-Verify, a system to verify the immigration status of employees.
DeSantis announced legislation to crack down on big tech and online censorship
DeSantis signed the Combating Public Disorder Act into law, which forbids intimidation by mobs and penalizes damage to historic properties or memorials.
Ron DeSantis signed Florida's anti-riot bill, which has been criticized for restricting civil rights and freedoms
Florida was set to ban 'deplatforming' on social media in a nod to Trump

Ron DeSantis signed the Fairness in Women's Sports Act, which bans transgender girls and women from participating and competing in middle-school and high-school girls' and college women's sports competitions.
A federal judge blocked the Florida law that would have prohibited tech companies from de-platforming political candidates, citing First Amendment and federal law violations.
DeSantis introduced a $5,000 signing bonus for Florida police officers to attract out-of-state police recruits.
Ron DeSantis opened a new fight with President Biden over immigration.
DeSantis voiced support for the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act, which prohibits discussion of sexual orientation or gender identity in school classrooms from kindergarten to grade 3.
Ron DeSantis signaled support for the 'Don't Say Gay' bill
DeSantis signed the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act into law, which took effect on July 1, 2022.
Florida's governor signed the 'Don't Say Gay' bill into law
Ron DeSantis signed a bill banning abortions after 15 weeks into law
Ron DeSantis signed a bill eliminating Walt Disney World's Reedy Creek district, after Disney opposed the 'Don't Say Gay' bill
A judge blocked Florida's 'Stop WOKE Act' pushed by Gov. DeSantis

DeSantis's administration recruited 50 newly arrived asylum seekers, mostly from Venezuela, and flew them to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts, in a move that the migrants later described as 'extreme and outrageous, and utterly intolerable in a civilized community'.
Ron DeSantis announced plans to expand the 'Don't Say Gay' law to all grades
Ron DeSantis signed a six-week abortion ban into law in a private late-night ceremony
Florida expanded the 'Don't Say Gay' rules to grades 4-12, banning lessons on sexual orientation and gender identity unless required by state standards
DeSantis announced plans to send over 1,000 personnel to Texas, including National Guard troops, to help Texas stem the influx of illegal immigration across the southern border.
DeSantis refused to accept $346 million from the Inflation Reduction Act for rebates to homeowners who want to retrofit their houses and make them more energy efficient.

Ron DeSantis signed into law a bill that requires social media platforms to prohibit people under 16 years old from making accounts, which has been criticized by digital rights organizations for infringing on Floridians' First Amendment rights.
A court overturned a large part of Florida's 'Don't Say Gay' law
A settlement was reached in the lawsuit between Disney and Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis' allies, regarding the 'Don't Say Gay' law and the Reedy Creek district
DeSantis vetoed a bill passed by the State House that would have created a statewide process to issue closures and send warnings if the bacteria in waterways reached unsafe levels.
State Attorney Jack Campbell opened a criminal investigation into allegations of money laundering and wire fraud involving Ron DeSantis's chief of staff and the Hope Florida Foundation.
A court ruled on Kayle B. Bates v. State of Florida & Kayle B. Bates v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a case involving the State of Florida under Ron DeSantis, on August 12, 2025.
A court ruled on Victor Tony Jones v. State of Florida & Victor Tony Jones v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a case involving the State of Florida under Ron DeSantis, on September 24, 2025.
A court ruled on McGraw v. DeSantis, a case involving Ron DeSantis, on September 26, 2025.
A court ruled on Michael Bernard Bell v. State of Florida, a case involving the state of Florida.
A court ruled on Edward J. Zakrzewski, II v. State of Florida, a case involving the state of Florida.
A court ruled on Bailey v. Laurie, DeSantis, a case involving the state of Florida.
